title: Wire real routing and per-route contextual buttons configs
description: Replace placeholder routing with real wiring and register a per-route TViewButtonsConfig with ContextualButtonsProvider so the top, bottom, and side chrome swap correctly as the visitor navigates between shop home, aisle detail, and every product detail.

context.what_is_left: After phase 4, every view exists but the router still resolves to placeholders in some paths, and the ContextualButtonsProvider at the root has no per-route button builders. This task is the 'glue' that connects the already-built pieces.
context.contextual_buttons_contract: ContextualButtonsProvider<ShowcaseRoute> takes a Map<ShowcaseRoute, TViewButtonsConfig<...>>. Each TViewButtonsConfig defines a mobile builder and a desktop builder. The shell's TContextualButtons host consumes whichever config the current route looks up, automatically resized by the TResponsiveBuilder device type.
per_route_chrome_rules: shop_home: top app bar title 'Turbo Widgets Shop' + theme toggle action; no back button; nav shows 'Shop' highlighted on desktop side rail and no highlight on mobile bottom bar's aisle tabs.
aisle_detail: top app bar title = aisle label + theme toggle + back button; nav shows that aisle highlighted.
product_detail: top app bar title = widget name + theme toggle + back button; nav shows the product's aisle highlighted.

goals: ShowcaseRouter resolves every ShowcaseRoute value to its real view (no placeholders left).
main.dart's ContextualButtonsProvider receives a complete Map<ShowcaseRoute, TViewButtonsConfig> covering every route.
Each TViewButtonsConfig defines separate mobile and desktop builders so that resizing the window visibly swaps the chrome.
Navigating between shop home, an aisle detail, and a product detail results in the correct title, back button, and nav highlight on both mobile and desktop window sizes.
No view receives placeholder content — the app is end-to-end complete after this task.
non_goals: Building any new view or view model.
acceptance_criteria: `flutter run` launches and all navigation flows work: shop home → aisle → product → back → aisle → back → shop home.
Resizing the window across the breakpoint swaps bottom nav ↔ side rail cleanly on every route.
Theme toggle works from every route.
Back button appears only on detail routes.
constraints: Route definitions live in one place — do not scatter route-to-view mappings across files.
The contextual buttons map is built in the DI composition root, not inside any view.
tools_to_use: RepoPrompt MCP for reading ContextualButtonsProvider, TViewButtonsConfig, TContextualButtonsConfig sources.

testing_and_verification.unit_tests: Write Gherkin-style unit tests verifying ShowcaseRouter's route-to-view resolution is complete: iterate every ShowcaseRoute enum value and assert the router returns a non-null view builder for each. This catches the common 'forgot to register a new route' bug when a new widget is added later. No mocks.
verification_gate: Manual end-to-end navigation verification across every route and both window sizes.

quality_checklist.maintainability: Adding a new widget means adding one enum value + one catalog entry + one router entry + a showcase view folder — nothing else.
quality_checklist.dry: Route registration is not duplicated between the router and the contextual buttons map — derive one from the other if practical, or centralise both behind the catalog service.
quality_checklist.single_responsibility: The router resolves routes. DI composition wires services. The shell renders. Do not conflate.
quality_checklist.performance: Contextual button builders are cheap — no heavy allocations per route lookup.

workflow_instructions: Read ContextualButtonsProvider and TViewButtonsConfig sources carefully — their exact shape determines the wiring.
Update 20-author-example-readme-playbook-task's `follow_up_notes` with the exact list of files a future contributor must touch when adding a new widget — the README task consumes this list.
